claim: A SETTLED requirement with enforcement ENFORCED shall be valid iff EITHER the engine mechanism (enforced_by non-empty) OR the authored mechanism (implemented_by AND verified_by both non-empty) names a real enforcer; neither present is a violation.
why: PLAN-authored-spec-discipline.md §5 (steward decision 2026-07-16) and §12 make the ENFORCED gate DISJUNCTIVE: a requirement may be SETTLED without code as honest roadmap debt, but ENFORCED requires a real noun via one of two paths -- the pre-existing engine mechanism (enforced_by: a check_* registry name or a repo-wide Test* function, guarded by check_enforced_names_invariant's non-emptiness half) or the new authored-spec mechanism (implemented_by AND verified_by both non-empty, pointing into a domain's own spec/ tree). check_enforced_names_invariant alone only recognizes the enforced_by half and would wrongly reject a requirement legitimately ENFORCED purely via implemented_by+verified_by; check_enforced_requires_enforcer_or_authored_link (internal/invariants/authored_links.go) is the widened disjunctive gate that accepts either path, so a self-domain requirement enforced by the engine's own check_*/Test* machinery is not forced to fabricate a redundant authored spec/ reference, and a business domain's authored-spec requirement is not forced to invent a fake engine-side enforcer. Having implemented_by without verified_by (or vice versa) does NOT satisfy the authored path -- "embodied somewhere" without "proven somewhere" is not a real guarantee. Mutation-proof coverage in internal/invariants/authored_links_test.go proves both positive paths (engine-only, authored-only) pass, and a round-trip mutation (neither -> implemented_by-only, still violating -> both fields present, clears) proves the disjunction is exact, not a silent OR-by-accident.
evidence: go test ./internal/invariants/... -run EnforcedRequiresEnforcerOrAuthoredLink -v: engine-path/authored-path/disjunctive mutation round-trip all pass (task #223 session, 2026-07-16)
